mappings.rationaleAI: Australian history covers early exploration (B.1), colonial foundations (B.2), White Australia policy and federation (B.3), and Aboriginal relations (B.4). Broader development addresses A.
Colonial empires address European exploration and colonial settlement (A.3).
British Empire addresses colonial settlement (A.3). Colonization connects to NSW settlement (B.2). Imperial governance provides context for British control of New Zealand (C.1).
Postcolonial perspectives address colonial settlement (A.3). Indigenous experience connects to Aboriginal relations (B.4) and Maori relations (C.2).
Cook's voyages address colonial settlement (A.3) and early exploration (B.1). Broader exploration connects to historiographic problems (A.1).
National self-determination connects to federation and commonwealth establishment (B.2).
Immigration patterns address colonial expansion (B.2) and White Australia policy (B.3).
World War I addresses Australia's cooperation with Britain (B.3).
Archaeological methods address prehistory of Australia and Oceania (A.2).
Ethnography addresses Oceanian ethnography (A.2). Non-Western societies connect to Aboriginal (B.4) and Maori (C.2) relations.
